53. Comply with the rules on warranties and all other related requirements provided in the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

63. Conduct inspection to vacant lots under the SSP and if found to be positioned by eligible individuals, execute INA and demand payment the cost of the lot.

In view of the foregoing, we restated our recommendation, and Management agreed that:

65. All items in the Annual Budgets of the Municipality, especially those under the 20 per cent DF, should be described in specific terms. If presented in lump sum, approval of the SB authorizing the LCE to charge the specific project from the lump sum appropriation, must be sought either a) before the commencement of the procurement process; or b) prior to signing of the contract for the specific project; and
66. Refrain from charging expenditures enumerated in Item 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 against the 20 per cent DF.

67. We recommended, and Management agreed to stop disbursing funds for expenditures that are not included in the priority projects, programs, and activities outlined in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC No. 2013-1, as well as RA No. 10121, so as not to diminish the fund’s intended purpose.

77. We recommended, and Management agreed to require the MGSO and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit a proposal to the Municipal Mayor for amendments to Municipal Ordinance No. 03, s. of 2023 to include complete descriptions of the equipment and the inclusion of certain equipment owned by the Municipality that is not currently covered by the rental rates as enumerated, and submit the approved proposal to the SB for adoption and approval.

78. We recommended for the Management to: Hire only Job Order workers within the approved budget so as not to compromise the funding requirements of the Municipality on the basis of necessity, reasonableness, and legal considerations; and
79. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to secure and submit to COA the ordinance re-aligning the funds in Personnel Services to MOOE for the noted amount.

80. We recommended for the Management to require the AOs/SDOs to maintain cashbooks (see Annex 21) separately for the different CAs granted to them, to comply with the requirements of Section 63(A)(3) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual and Section 28, Chapter 1, of the MNGAS for LGUs.
81. We recommended for the Management to: Review and properly identity PPAs to be funded out of the LCPC funds; and

82. Charge only those expenditures that are included in the approved annual budget otherwise cause a reprogramming of funds to accommodate those which are not included with due consideration to the purpose for setting up of LCPC funds.

We recommended for the Management to require the Solid Waste Management Board through the focal person to follow up the application for approval of the proposed landfill site and thereby secure the required ECC on the site or area used by the Municipality for solid waste disposal, to comply with Section 48(14), Chapter VI of RA No. 9003.

85. We recommended for the Management to direct the Solid Waste Management Board through the responsible person to implement the construction of the building facility for the DOST ITDI Bioreactor and Shredder for Organic Waste Materials to upgrade the Solid Waste Management Facility and to promote efficiency, economy, and effectiveness in the utilization and management of the Municipality’s resources.

86. Moreover, we restated our CY 2021 recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to augment current continuing appropriation of P100,000.00 for the building construction POW of P0.778 million to house the DOST ITDI Bioreactor and Shredder for Organic Waste Materials.

87. We recommended for the Management to direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) to convene to review the provisions of Section 17(a) and (b), Article XVI, of Municipal Ordinance No.16. s. 2019 and adopt specific revenue-generating measures to promote the viability of its Solid Waste Management Plan.

88. We recommended for Management, through the MSWMB to: a) Convene and direct the attention of the respective barangays, through the ABC President as one of the members of the ISWMB, to establish/ operate/ repair/ label the MRFs under their jurisdiction to comply with Section 7, Article VII, in relation to Section 7.5(a) and (d), Article VIII of Municipal Ordinance No. 16, s. 2019.

89. b) Develop a strategy on how to effectively impose the fines and penalties provided under Section 20, Article XVII, for the prohibited acts committed as listed in Section 18, Article XVII of Municipal Ordinance No. 16, s. 2019.

90. We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the collections intact and within the prescribed period as provided for by Section 88(A) of the Local Treasury Operations Manual, which reiterates Section 32, Chapter 3, of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

91. Prepare internal rules in the adoption of electronic signatures in accordance with COA Circular No. 2021-006 that include, among others, the following: Designation of focal person for all matters pertaining to electronic signing implementation of the LGU;
92. Development, maintenance, and updating of the system documentation used for creating electronic records with signatures;

93. Development, maintenance, and implementation of standard operating procedures for the creation, utilization, storage, security, and management of electronic records that contain signatures, to ensure that the records are protected from unauthorized alteration or destruction; Implementation of security awareness program such as training the employees on the acceptable use of signature on electronic documents; and
94. Guidelines with regard to the scope of the employee’s authority to use electronic documents; and sanction for misuse or abuse of signatures.

95. To strengthen the risk management capabilities of the Municipal Government and enable it to manage its vulnerabilities in the event of disruptive events, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor shall  institutionalize the PSCP by creating a working group that will perform the following activities:
a. to conduct risk assessment;
b. to identify critical processes and functions;
c. to determine scenarios that may disrupt normal operation;
d. to conduct risk analysis and impact analysis;

e. to formulate the PSCP based on the identified risks on critical processes and functions and its related steps to be followed to eliminate, if not mitigate, the impact of the determined disruptions following the prescribed template and be guided by the PSCP guidebook; and
96. To submit plan to the MDRRMO for review, to the Municipal Mayor for approval, and to the SB for the passage of a resolution to institutionalize the PSCP.

97. Management be informed and updated of the relevant issuances by authorities that may affect their local governance performance scorecards like the DILG’s Seal of Good Local Governance and comply with the provisions therein like the submission of PSCP.

98. We recommended for Management to: Direct the Municipal Engineering Office to prepare and maintain a monthly monitoring report to keep track of the progress of all PPAs especially those that involved infrastructure projects within its jurisdiction from planning to its completion stage;
99. Assign Project Heads/Managers to all non-infrastructure PPAs to prepare and consolidate a Monthly Monitoring Report;

100. Direct the Municipal Engineering Office and the assigned Project Heads/Managers to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant on the costs incurred in each PPAs as basis in the preparation of their Monthly Monitoring Report which should be submitted to COA at least on a quarterly basis;
101. Require the Municipal Engineering Office and the assigned Project Heads/Manager to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004.

102. Management to assess the possibility of granting compensation-related Magna Carta Benefits to PSWs by crafting internal guidelines providing specific criteria and administrative procedures on the grant of each Magna Carta benefit; and, if found justifiable, provide appropriation for qualified recipients in the ensuing year to implement DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No. 1 s.2018.

Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

112.          The Municipal Mayor issue an Executive or Administrative Order creating the Monitoring and Evaluation Team in consonance with the provisions of item 5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in order to implement the GAD PPAs efficiently and effectively.

113. The noted deficiency as stated in the Hydro-Geological Assessment Report by complying with their recommendation. We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Designate a statistician or at least a focal person assigned for CBMS data collection, preservation and safekeeping;
Allocate budget in the succeeding year for the preparation or acquisition of an information system specifically for storing CBMS data; and
Conduct synchronized data collection of CBMS every three years.

Recommendations
20.7 We recommended, and the Management agreed that the Local Finance Committee:
Review the CY 2024 continuing appropriations and advise the MBO to make necessary adjustments in the SAAOB to reduce the appropriation by P1.405 million due to absence of cash back-up as a result of unrealized collections;
Review the balances of continuing appropriations of  P29.972 million, as part of the annual budget preparation and the sanggunian concerned may approve, upon recommendation of the LCE, the reversion of funds no longer needed in connection with the activities funded by said continuing appropriations subject to the provisions of this section; and
Carefully project a more realistic revenue target based on historical data and other corroborating factors, like marketability, weather, or any other conditions, to come up with an attainable estimate and to ensure that planned PPAs are supported by available cash.

Recommendations
We recommended, and the Management agreed that the MBO:
Prepare, maintain, and update its registries on obligations per allotment class;
Ensure that appropriation is sufficient prior to certification to the existence of appropriations in the CAFOA/OBR; and
In cases of lack of appropriation, proper procedures in the realignment/augmentation/supplemental budget shall be made before disbursements of funds.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Refrain from re-appropriating/transferring unexpended balances as source of funds for non-20 per cent DF;
Return the P3.775 million to the 20 per cent DF and reappropriate the same  to finance only those development PPAs identified to be funded under the DF for the ensuing year;
Faithfully adhere to the budgetary and programming policies and procedures whenever there are changes to the 20 per cent DF budget, and ensure that it is supported by MDC Resolutions and approval of SB, and/or appropriate Ordinances in cases of reversions/savings, and certification from the MBO and Treasurer of available savings; and
Ensure that the sources of funds available for appropriations are certified under oath by the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant and attested by the LCE.
We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The MBO update their SAAOB and reconcile their records with the Municipal Accountant to reflect the correct balances of LDRRMF as at a given period; and
Refrain from transferring the LDRRMF Budget as source of funds for other unrelated purposes and strictly maintain the 5 per cent estimated revenue of LGUs from regular sources to adequately address disasters and emergencies, potentially endangering lives and property.
We recommended, and Management agreed to refrain from transferring the LCPC Budget as source of funds for other unrelated purposes in accordance with Section 3, Article XV of the Philippine Constitution and RA No. 9344, known as the “Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006”.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 22 of RA No. 10121, COA Circular No. 2012-002, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, Policies and Procedures on the Utilization of the LDRRMF by restraining from releasing and using the QRF to only be utilized for relief and recovery projects and activities in actual calamities and emergencies, supported by SB Resolution and the Declaration of State of Calamity.

Recommendation
We recommended, and Management agreed to stop disbursing funds for expenditures that are not included in the priority PPAs outlined in Section 5 of NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC No. 2013-1, as well as RA No. 10121, so as not to diminish the fund’s intended purpose.

Recommendations
We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure the optimum utilization of the 20 per cent DF by:
Fully implementing programmed development PPAs during the year;
Continuously monitoring the implementation of these planned PPAs to attain the desired benefits as envisioned in DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020; and
Requiring monthly reconciliation of Accounting and Budget records to reflect more timely and accurate reports.

Recommendation
In view of the foregoing, we restated our recommendation and Management agreed that PPAs be funded by the 20 per cent DF are described in specific terms. If lump sum appropriations cannot be avoided, approval of the SB authorizing the LCE to charge the specific project from the lump sum appropriation must be sought either a) before the commencement of the procurement process; or b) prior to signing of the contract for the specific project.

The Municipal Mayor through the Municipal Accountant or its authorized representative to submit immediately all the cash advance Disbursement Vouchers charged to the municipality’s Confidential Fund for transactions that transpired in CY 2017. We reiterated our recommendation that the concerned municipal officers and employees be directed by the Local Chief Executive to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances. Management shall create an inventory committee by PPE type, with the end view of establishing the correctness of the reported balances as well as the existence, condition and custodianship of the municipal assets and submit the RPCI and RCPPE not later than January 31 of each year to enable reconciliation with the accounting and property records and corrections of verified discrepancies; and The Local Chief Executive shall instruct the MGSO Officer in the conduct of inventory per Department and Office of the Municipality in compliance with the above-stated provisions of law.

Management carefully plan and monitor the utilization of its 20 per cent Development Fund. It is recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council shall prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the attached format duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit the same to the COA as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular 2014-1 dated April 14, 2014.

The concerned municipal officials observe and strictly adhere to all the requirements of the pertinent provisions of the cited Joint Circular, particularly the documentary requisites in the granting of cash advances from the Municipality’s Confidential Fund.

The Local School Board (LSB) refrain from charging expenditures which are in the nature of regular MOOE of various schools to the Special Education Fund. Management cease the practice of paying monthly government share contribution beyond the maximum limit of ₱100.00 per employee, as provided by Section1, Rule VI of RA 9679. Employees who opt to continue its personal contribution of 2 per cent may do so, provided that the government share remain at a maximum of ₱100.00.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections as required under Section 69 of PD 1445 and Section 62 C of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

Require the Municipal Accountant to include the Trust Fund bank accounts in performing bank reconciliation on a monthly basis and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor in compliance to Section 74 of PD 1445.

Management shall see to it that deductions from payroll shall not result to a net take home pay of less than the amount mandated under the provision of the General Appropriations Act for a given year. Likewise, management shall adopt a strategy that would immediately detect the net take home pay of its personnel whenever loan application is made, and not to approve the application of the result thereof would reduce the net pay below the statutory requirement.
The Municipality shall relocate its dumpsite in an area ideal for its location as mandated by RA 9003 and its implementing rules.

The Municipal Accountant be required to prepare the bank reconciliation statement for all funds and prepare it regularly in accordance with Sec. 74 of PD 1445 and the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011. Any difference between the cash balance per bank versus the book balance must be traced and reconciled to affect the accurate cash in bank balance.

Require the Municipal Assessor to submit the certified assessment roll to the Municipal Treasurer in order for her to submit to the Municipal Accountant the certified list of tax payers at the beginning of the year in order for the latter to book up the estimated receivables of RPT and SET.

The designated disbursing officer should not be assigned with functions that would conflict its current position such as the handling of collections. Disbursing and collection functions should be given to different personnel since these are considered incompatible functions. Likewise, management should implement control measures to strengthen the internal control system of the municipality.

We recommended that concerned Municipal Officials should submit immediately the lacking documents as enumerated for auditorial and technical review of contracts of different projects implemented by the municipality. Also, perfected contracts with its corresponding supporting documents should be submitted to COA within 5 working days from the execution thereof. Likewise, stop the practice of submission of contracts with incomplete documents to facilitate early auditorial and technical review.

given  to  different  personnel  since  these  are  considered  incompatible
functions.  Likewise,  management  should  implement  control  measures   to
strengthen the internal control system of the municipality.

Recommendation:

       We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  through  the  Municipal
Accountant or its authorized representative to submit  immediately  all  the
cash  advance  Disbursement   Vouchers   charged   to   the   municipality’s
Confidential Fund for transactions that transpired  in  CY  2017.  Likewise,
the Municipal Accountant shall also ensure that all DVs will  have  complete
required supporting documents as enumerated  in  COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND  Joint
Circular No. 2015-01.

      Moreover, the Municipal Mayor  should  also  strictly  adhere  to  the
provisions of the same joint circular on the release and utilization  of  CF
on current and future transactions.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that  the  Municipality  through  the  Municipal  Mayor
should require strict compliance in allocating 1% of  its  Internal  Revenue
Allotment (IRA) as provided for under Section 15 of RA 9344  and  to  ensure
the establishment of LGU’s Local Council for the Protection of  Children  to
spearhead the adoption of a comprehensive plan  on  delinquency  prevention,
and to oversee its proper implementation.

Recommendation:

      We  recommended  that  concerned  Municipal  Officials  should  submit
immediately  the  lacking  documents  as  enumerated  for   auditorial   and
technical review of contracts  of  different  projects  implemented  by  the
municipality. Also, perfected contracts with  its  corresponding  supporting
documents should be  submitted  to  COA  within  5  working  days  from  the
execution thereof. Likewise, stop the practice of  submission  of  contracts
with incomplete documents  to  facilitate  early  auditorial  and  technical
review.

Recommendation:

      We recommended that the  municipality  thru  the  Municipal  Mayor  to
transfer the area for its garbage disposal  while  rehabilitating  the  open
dumpsite to prevent the posing of environmental and  health  hazards  within
the community where the area belongs. Moreover, the  LGU  shall  discontinue
the use of open dumpsite and allocate some resources to create  at  least  a
controlled dumpsite for solid waste disposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC Municipal Accountant be required to prepare the bank reconciliation statement for the General Fund and prepare it regularly in accordance with Sec. 74 of PD 1445 and the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011.
observations.reason: The newly assumed municipal accountant is having difficulty reconciling previous year’s balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee shall complete the conduct of its inventory to all PPE items using the RCPPE form as prescribed by the above mentioned provision. After the physical inventory, the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Committee shall properly reconcile the accounting records against the (Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment) RPCPPE to correctly reflect the balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The MAO will coordinate with the Mayor’s Office to create an inventory committee

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned office be required to comply with the provisions as required by the provisions of PD 1445, Volume I of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual and COA Circular No 2010-001.
observations.reason: Delay is mainly caused by the limited number of personnel in the Accounting Department of the Municipality

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality shall allocate at least five percent (5%) out of its total budget appropriation for the year in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 for Gender and Development Programs, Projects and Activities (GAD PPAs) that would promote gender equality within the locality.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference, management admitted that it only drafted a GAD Plan for the year 2016. There have been programs implemented in 2015 but management was unsure if they were GAD-related. Nonetheless, management obliged to comply with the team’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that physical inventory be conducted yearly by Management to establish the existence, validity and accuracy of the PPE recorded in the books of accounts. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintains the PPE ledger cards for each category of assets and the real property ledger cards for land to support the ledger balances of the PPE accounts.

We recommended Management direct the Accounting and Treasury Sections to: exhaust all possible means to locate the supporting documents pertaining to the Receivables accounts particularly long outstanding and significant balance, to support the validity of subject account; exert efforts to collect all valid claims particularly other receivables and refer collection cases to the Legal Office for appropriate action; and Restructure accounting records and ensure that all accounts are adequately supported with valid documents.

We  reiterated  our  recommendation  that  the  concerned   municipal
officers  and  employees  be  directed  by  the  Local  Chief  Executive  to
immediately  settle  their  unliquidated  cash   advances.   If   warranted,
withholding of salaries of those who failed to comply with the  requirements
must be imposed. It is also suggested  that  Management  shall  continue  to
send out demand letters to concerned personnel at their last  known  address
in preparation  for  the  request  for  write-off  in  accordance  with  COA
Circular No. 2016-005 and if warranted  after  thorough  evaluation  by  the
audit team.

We  recommend  that  the  municipality  shall  strictly   follow   the
transitory provisions on the complete recognition of Local Road  Network  as
stated in Commission on Audit Circular No. 2015-008 and that  the  concerned
municipal  employees  shall  observe  their  respective  functions  for  the
attainment of the same.

In view of the following observations, we recommend that:

   1. Management shall create an inventory committee by PPE  by  type,  with
      the end view of establishing the correctness of the reported  balances
      as well as the existence, condition and custodianship of the municipal
      assets and submit the RPCI and RCPPE not later than January 31 of each
      year to enable reconciliation with the accounting and property records
      and corrections of verified discrepancies; and


   2. The Local Chief Executive shall  instruct  the  MGSO  Officer  in  the
      conduct of inventory per Department and Office of the Municipality  in
      compliance with the above-stated provisions of law.

We  recommend  that  Management  carefully  plan   and   monitor   the
utilization of its 20%  Development  Fund.  Travel  expenses  or  any  other
expenses explicitly prohibited under JMC No.  2011-1  must  not  be  charged
against the said fund. Management must see to it that  only  those  priority
development projects enumerated in the same circular will  be  sourced  from
the 20% DF.

It  is  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Disaster   Risk   Reduction
Management Council shall prepare the Report on Sources  and  Utilization  of
DRRMF using the prescribed format duly certified correct  by  the  Municipal
Accountant and submit the same to the COA as  required  under  COA  Circular
No.  2012-002  dated  September  12,  2012  and  NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC   Joint
Memorandum Circular 2014-1 dated April 14, 2014.

We recommend that the concerned municipal officials observe  and
strictly adhere to all the requirements of the pertinent provisions  of  the
cited  Joint  Circular,  particularly  the  documentary  requisites  in  the
granting of cash advances from the Municipality’s Confidential Fund.

We recommend that the Local School Board (LSB) refrain  from  charging
expenditures which are in the nature of regular MOOE of various  schools  to
the Special Education Fund. Instead, allocate or use such fund to align  its
programs/projects/activities with the provisions and  objectives  set  forth
by the provisions of RA 7160 and RA 5447.

We recommend that the Local School  Board  (LSB)  carefully  plan  and
monitor the utilization of the Special Education Fund entrusted to  it,  and
judiciously  align  its  programs/projects/activities   with   the   diverse
priority activities embodied in RA 5447 in  order  to  sufficiently  address
the more pressing educational concerns in its locality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management should require the Petty Cash Custodian to immediately liquidate the outstanding balance as of December 31, 2015 and direct strict compliance thereat in accordance with Section 48, Volume I of New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs and monitor compliance by the accountable officers concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should make the necessary adjusting entry to take up the accumulated depreciation for the rest of the agency’s depreciable class of PPE for which depreciation were not taken up previously and thereafter provide monthly depreciation in compliance with the provisions stated in Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards 17.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the OIC-Municipal Accountant’s Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office: 1. to cause the time and full remittance to the BIR, GSIS, Pag-ibig and Philhealth taxes/contributions withheld.  Any penalty arising from the delay shall be the personal liability of both and other officials concerned. 2. to reconcile the accounts from prior years transactions to reflect the correct balances of accounts.

We recommended that management to furnish Auditor with copy of POs/contracts and other documents within five (5) working days after their perfection regardless of amount, for this office to have early information of the agency’s procurement and that management will comply with the provisions as stated in COA Circular No. 82-195 and COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the municipality shall implement immediately the Ecological Solid Waste Management system such as segregation at source, segregated collection and establishment of Materials Recovery Facility/s, recycling and composting method.
observations.managementAction: There have been programs implemented in 2015 but management was unsure if they were GAD-related. Nonetheless, management obliged to comply with the team’s recommendation.
